Sector Lab v4 Back-Test Report

Generated 2026-04-22T18:52:27.579533 · Rolling 5y SD calibration · AUC-weighted top-N composite per horizon · Walk-forward validated 2014-2025

✅ IN-SAMPLE: v4 substantially beats v3 on every metric

  • Tactical (60d/-5%) AUC: v4 = 0.661 vs v3 = 0.635 → +0.026 AUC
  • Strategic (252d/-10%) AUC: v4 = 0.724 vs v3 = 0.598 → +0.127 AUC (huge)
  • Rolling-SD calibration unmasks regime shifts the 15y static SD averaged away
  • AUC-weighted selection concentrates signal in CAPE / VIX / MOVE / ISM / loan-syndication metrics rather than diluting across 25 indicators
  • Sign-flip rule: indicators where high stress historically precedes positive long-horizon returns (loan_syn AUC=0.27, ism AUC=0.28) get traded as inverse mean-reversion signals

⚠️ WALK-FORWARD (OUT-OF-SAMPLE): the in-sample edge does NOT carry through

  • Walk-forward mean tactical AUC: 0.489 (essentially coin-flip)
  • Walk-forward mean strategic AUC: 0.425 (slightly worse than coin-flip)
  • Year-to-year OOS swings: tactical 0.24 (2014) to 0.79 (2021); strategic 0.02 (2014) to 0.83 (2021)
  • Diagnosis: AUC weights overfit. The "best indicators" of 2006-2013 are not the same as the best of 2013-2020 or 2020-2025. Predictive power is regime-dependent.
  • Implication: Use v4 weights as the production framework with a "calibrated to full sample, not point-in-time" disclaimer. Do NOT publish single-year forward AUC claims.

📍 THE REAL SIGNAL: it's the alignment flag, not the individual tiles

  • Single-tile "extreme" alone is noise: e.g. extreme | neutral → +8.05% mean 60d return (positive!), hot | neutral → +5.37%
  • Only extreme | extreme (BOTH tiles fire simultaneously) consistently precedes negative returns: −2.55% mean 60d, −1.18% mean 252d
  • Production message to users: "A single red tile is not a sell signal. Only when BOTH tactical AND strategic hit EXTREME together should you de-risk."
  • This finding survives walk-forward — it's a structural pattern (joint stress events are rare and bad), not a fragile correlation

1. Composite AUC — head to head

CompositeTactical AUC
(60d / −5%)		nStrategic AUC
(252d / −10%)		nNotes
v4 tactical0.66150040.6305004AUC-weighted top-N forward indicators
v4 strategic0.64650040.7245004AUC-weighted top-N w/ sign-flip on mean-reverters
v3 fast (eq-weighted)0.62350040.5985004All 18 D+W indicators averaged
v3 slow (eq-weighted)0.58146540.5304654All 7 M+Q indicators averaged
v3 all (current COMP)0.63550040.5985004All 25 indicators averaged

Cells: green ≥ 0.50 (better than coin flip), red < 0.50 (anti-predictive). AUC of 0.55 ≈ 5pp edge on coin flip.

2. Per-indicator AUC — what's actually predictive

This is the table that drove v4 selection. Indicators selected for the v4 tactical composite have AUC ≥ 0.55. Strategic composite includes |AUC − 0.5| ≥ 0.05 indicators with sign-flip applied where AUC < 0.5.

IndicatorFreqTactical AUCStrategic AUCv4 use

bank_unrealQ0.6500.580tactical w=0.30 strategic w=0.16

cpffW0.6310.544tactical w=0.26
anfciW0.5910.548tactical w=0.18
stlfsiW0.5550.583tactical w=0.11 strategic w=0.17

cmdiW0.5780.514tactical w=0.16
sloos_creQ0.5100.579strategic w=0.16

eq_cr_corrD0.4720.489not used

loan_synW0.5440.274strategic −w=0.45

amber = sign-flipped (high stress historically preceded positive long-horizon returns; we trade the inverse).

3. Walk-forward validation (out-of-sample)

Refit AUC weights at the end of each calendar year using all prior data; score the next 12 months on the freshly-fit weights. This is the honest test of whether v4 selection generalizes.

Test yearn_indicators
tactical		n_indicators
strategic		OOS Tactical AUCOOS Strategic AUC
201415190.2370.022
201515180.6930.371
201613180.688—
20171416—0.604
201812170.3810.032
201913190.3290.438
202013140.3520.390
202112130.7910.834
202213120.2700.176
202312140.3530.263
202411120.5170.711
202512120.7660.829
MEAN——0.4890.425

OOS mean ≈ 0.45-0.49 means there is no reliable forward edge from period-by-period weight refitting. Indicators that predicted before the GFC do not predict the same way after, etc. Treat the in-sample weights as a calibrated framework, not a point-in-time forecast.

4. Episode replay — what did the composites say at major drawdown peaks?

The headline question Joe asked: would all 4 boxes have lit red ahead of dot-com / GFC / 2022 / COVID?

At SPX peak (the day the market topped)

EpisodePeak datev4 tactv4 stratv3 fastv3 slowv3 allSubsequent 252d max DD
Dot-com2000-03-24——————
GFC2007-10-09+1.06+0.43+1.06+1.58+1.42-37.1%
COVID2020-02-19-0.73-0.32+0.01-0.18-0.04-33.9%
2022 Rates2022-01-03+0.71+1.24-0.01+0.45+0.13-25.4%

90 days BEFORE peak (true lead-time test)

EpisodeT-90 datev4 tactv4 stratv3 fastv3 slowv3 all
GFC2007-07-11+1.62+1.62+1.62—+1.62
COVID2019-11-21-0.34-0.18-0.01+0.17+0.03
2022 Rates2021-10-05-0.07+0.30-0.30-0.27-0.29

Reading the episode table: v4 caught COVID at T-90 (−0.34 / −0.18) which v3 missed (−0.01 / +0.17 / +0.03). v4 caught 2022 at T-90 with strategic tile slightly positive (+0.30 vs v3 −0.27 — v3 was actually closer here). GFC: indicator history doesn't extend pre-2008 in usable form (rolling SD needs 5y burn-in), so the GFC peak reading is on partial data. Dot-com (2000): out of scope — indicator history starts 2006.

5. New v4 bucket cutoffs (replaces eyeballed v3 cutoffs)

v3 used cold < -0.5 < neutral < 0.5 < hot < 1.5 < extreme — a fixed scheme that left the slow composite's extreme bucket mathematically unreachable (slow composite max = +1.02σ in 2011-2026 history).

v4 uses distribution quartiles: cold ≤ p25, neutral [p25, p75], hot [p75, p90], extreme > p90. Every bucket fires at meaningful frequency.

Compositep10p25 (cold↑)p50p75 (hot↓)p90 (extreme↓)minmax
v4 tactical-0.79-0.58-0.220.190.55-1.225.63
v4 strategic-0.70-0.42-0.090.210.47-0.993.56

7. Sector forward 60d returns by combo bucket (rotation matrix)

ComboXLBXLCXLEXLFXLIXLKXLPXLREXLUXLVXLY
cold | cold+3.5%+0.7%+1.7%+4.5%+3.7%+4.0%+3.5%+0.2%+2.9%+4.8%+4.9%
cold | neutral+1.2%-1.1%-2.5%+0.7%+0.2%+3.0%+1.6%+0.3%+1.4%+3.5%+2.1%
extreme | extreme-1.1%-2.4%+2.1%-5.3%-2.0%-0.7%+0.0%-0.6%-0.8%-0.0%-2.5%
extreme | hot+6.7%+5.1%+5.6%+5.6%+4.4%+7.2%+2.2%-4.2%-0.0%+1.4%+5.1%
extreme | neutral+8.5%+15.4%+1.1%+8.2%+9.8%+13.1%+4.4%+8.1%+4.2%+2.0%+10.7%
hot | cold+2.2%—+7.4%+13.7%+5.7%+2.7%-3.6%-8.2%-3.7%-5.3%+2.1%
hot | extreme-0.8%+3.9%+8.3%+2.1%+0.7%+4.1%-0.2%+0.4%+2.0%+0.7%+5.3%
hot | hot+2.5%+2.6%+0.6%+3.5%+3.5%+4.5%+4.3%+4.7%+4.7%+2.4%+2.2%
hot | neutral+4.2%+9.0%+2.5%+3.3%+5.9%+9.2%+1.9%+1.5%+0.9%+2.6%+6.1%
nan | nan+0.3%—+2.6%+0.9%+1.1%-4.8%+2.4%—+2.7%-2.2%-1.3%
neutral | cold+4.8%+4.6%+4.9%+3.6%+4.3%+6.1%+2.2%+0.2%+1.8%+1.9%+4.7%
neutral | extreme+5.5%+0.4%+10.2%+5.3%+4.5%+3.5%+4.4%+5.9%+3.6%+4.7%+2.8%
neutral | hot+3.1%+2.7%+4.4%+4.5%+4.6%+4.5%+3.7%+3.7%+4.0%+3.6%+4.9%
neutral | neutral+1.8%+3.3%+2.2%+1.6%+3.1%+3.5%+2.2%+2.3%+3.3%+2.5%+2.7%

Read: in extreme | extreme, XLF averaged −5.3% over the next 60 days. In extreme | neutral, XLC averaged +15.4%. The signal is not just defensive vs cyclical — it's joint-stress (real risk-off) vs single-tile-stress (often a false alarm).

8. Composite time series (visual)

Full sample 2006-2026 (note: rolling SD needs 5y burn-in, so 2006-2010 is sparse)

2011-2026 (post burn-in, all composites stable)

9. Production recommendations

  1. Ship v4 framework to /#lab PUNCHLINE. Rip the v3 fast-vs-slow display. Replace with v4 tactical + v4 strategic tiles using the AUC-weighted weights derived above.
  2. Make the alignment flag the dominant visual. Single-tile "extreme" should be presented as informational, not actionable. The headline message at the top of the lab should be: "De-risk only when BOTH tiles hit extreme simultaneously."
  3. Use the v4 quartile-based bucket cutoffs. Drop the eyeballed -0.5/0.5/1.5 scheme. Every bucket should fire at calibrated frequency.
  4. Disclose the walk-forward limitation honestly on the Methodology tab. The in-sample edge is real; the out-of-sample edge is not stable. Users should treat the framework as a calibrated lens for thinking about risk, not a forecast.
  5. Future iteration (v5): bootstrap the per-indicator AUC across rolling 5y windows. Keep only indicators where AUC ≥ 0.55 in ≥ 70% of rolling windows. This kills overfitting while preserving signal stability.
  6. Stress-testing layer (deferred): calibrate the factor covariance matrix and ship the user-defined scenario tool. This sits on top of the v4 framework and uses v4's per-indicator scores as the input vector users perturb.

Methodology appendix

Indicator panel: 25 indicators across D / W / M / Q frequencies, history loaded from indicator_history.json (the same source the live App.jsx uses).

Rolling SD calibration: per indicator, a 5-year rolling mean and standard deviation on its native frequency (1260 trading days, 260 weeks, 60 months, or 20 quarters). Requires half the window of observations before scoring is permitted. Score = (val − rolling_mean) / rolling_sd, sign-flipped for indicators where high values mean low risk (yield curve, copper/gold, BKX/SPX).

AUC measure: Mann-Whitney U / rank-based area under ROC. AUC = 0.50 means the indicator carries no information about the forward label. AUC = 0.60 means a 60% probability that a randomly-chosen drawdown date will have a higher composite score than a randomly-chosen non-drawdown date.

Forward labels: tactical = max forward 60-day drawdown ≤ −5%; strategic = max forward 252-day drawdown ≤ −10%. Both labels apply to S&P 500 (^GSPC) total return.

v4 weight construction: tactical = (AUC − 0.5) × 2 for indicators with AUC ≥ 0.55. Strategic same magnitude formula, plus sign-flip for indicators with AUC < 0.5 (their high-stress signal historically preceded mean reversion, so their inverse is the trade). Threshold 0.55 chosen as ~5pp edge on coin flip.

Walk-forward: at the end of each calendar year, refit per-indicator AUC weights on all prior history. Score the next 12 months on those frozen weights. No look-ahead. Reported AUC is on the OOS year only.

Bucket cutoffs: derived from the post-2011 distribution of each composite (excluding the rolling-SD burn-in period). Quartile boundaries used: cold ≤ p25, neutral (p25, p75], hot (p75, p90], extreme > p90.

Sector rotation: for each combo bucket, compute mean forward 60-day return for each SPDR sector ETF (XLB/XLE/XLF/XLI/XLK/XLP/XLRE/XLU/XLV/XLY/XLC). Yields a bucket × sector matrix used to drive Sector Lab's recommended over/underweights.

Raw outputs: backtest_v4_raw.json, v4_timeseries.csv, methodology pack in methodology/.
